references.content: | Behavioral Drift | HHI-BEH-001 | MEASURE | Drift detection alignment |
Behavioral Drift (HHI-BEH-001) is the gradual divergence between expected and observed system behavior as interaction patterns accumulate across time.
| HHI-BEH-001 | Behavioral Accumulation | Behavioral Dynamics | System Behavior | MAP 1.5 | Clause 6.1.3 | Art. 9(2)(a) | Principle 4 |

references.content: Override Erosion (HHI-INT-003) is the gradual decline in human willingness to override automated system decisions, often driven by Reliance Formation (HHI-BEH-002) and Confidence Reinforcement (HHI-INT-002).
| HHI-BEH-002 | Behavioral Drift | Behavioral Dynamics | System Behavior | MEASURE 2.6 | Clause 9.2 | Art. 9(2)(b) | Principle 5 |
